fiveWH.WHAT: .eslintrc module
fiveWH.WHY: Part of CORE region
fiveWH.HOW: Auto-aligned by LEEWAY align-agent

fiveWH.WHAT: Example of a LEEWAY-compliant custom agent implementation
fiveWH.WHY: Demonstrates how third-party agents integrate with the LEEWAY SDK governance model
fiveWH.HOW: ESM module implementing a simple LEEWAY agent contract with run() and describe() methods

fiveWH.WHAT: Nexus voice control button — the primary interface for voice commands
fiveWH.WHY: Provides users with a single clearly-identified entry point for voice interactions
fiveWH.HOW: React functional component with Tailwind CSS styling and accessibility support

fiveWH.WHAT: Architecture map agent — produces a visual or JSON architecture map of the codebase
fiveWH.WHY: Understanding system architecture is essential for safe evolution and AI-assisted development
fiveWH.HOW: Reads LEEWAY registry and builds a layered region/module graph in JSON and ASCII

fiveWH.WHAT: Docs agent — generates structured plain-English docs from LEEWAY headers and registries
fiveWH.WHY: Human-readable documentation must be automatically derivable from code headers
fiveWH.HOW: Reads LEEWAY headers and produces Markdown documentation summaries

fiveWH.WHAT: Explain agent — explains a file, module, or service in plain English
fiveWH.WHY: Any file in a LEEWAY system must be self-explainable to any developer or AI tool
fiveWH.HOW: Reads LEEWAY headers and produces structured human-readable explanations

fiveWH.WHAT: Intent registry agent — tracks supported intents and their routes
fiveWH.WHY: AI agents and voice assistants need a machine-readable registry of supported intents
fiveWH.HOW: Maintains a registry of intent-to-handler mappings in .leeway/intents.json

fiveWH.WHAT: Schema agent — generates machine-readable schemas and capability metadata
fiveWH.WHY: Applications must expose structured data for AI tools, search engines, and voice assistants
fiveWH.HOW: Generates Schema.org JSON-LD, OpenGraph, and capability manifest from LEEWAY metadata

fiveWH.WHAT: Sitemap agent — builds site and discovery maps from LEEWAY registry
fiveWH.WHY: Applications must expose sitemaps for crawlers, search engines, and AI indexers
fiveWH.HOW: Reads route registrations and LEEWAY headers to build XML and JSON sitemaps

fiveWH.WHAT: Align agent — normalizes files, settings, transports, ports, and env structure
fiveWH.WHY: Drift in naming, structure, and configuration breaks governance and causes deployment failures
fiveWH.HOW: Reads assessment results, proposes normalized corrections, applies non-destructive patches

fiveWH.WHAT: Assess agent — surveys what already exists before anything is created or patched
fiveWH.WHY: Prevents duplicate work, identifies existing patterns, and gives align/audit agents accurate ground truth
fiveWH.HOW: Event-driven file walker, dormant until invoked, returns structured inventory

fiveWH.WHAT: Audit agent — scores compliance, generates reports, and blocks unsafe changes
fiveWH.WHY: LEEWAY systems must be continuously validated to remain stable and governable
fiveWH.HOW: Aggregates compliance scores across all files, produces JSON and text reports

fiveWH.WHAT: Circular dependency agent — detects circular imports and risky graph patterns
fiveWH.WHY: Circular imports cause module initialization failures and memory leaks
fiveWH.HOW: DFS cycle detection on the dependency graph produced by dependency-graph-agent

fiveWH.WHAT: Dependency graph agent — builds lightweight dependency maps between modules
fiveWH.WHY: Understanding module dependencies is essential for safe refactoring and change impact analysis
fiveWH.HOW: Parses import/require statements, builds directed adjacency graph

fiveWH.WHAT: Duplicate logic agent — detects repeated logic and duplicate injected blocks
fiveWH.WHY: Duplicate code wastes space, confuses developers, and creates maintenance debt
fiveWH.HOW: Hash-based block comparison across files, sliding window on function bodies

fiveWH.WHAT: Import agent — normalizes import style and validates local import rules
fiveWH.WHY: Mixed import styles and broken paths cause module resolution failures
fiveWH.HOW: Scans import statements for style violations and common path issues

fiveWH.WHAT: Module policy agent — prevents CommonJS/ESM drift within a project
fiveWH.WHY: Mixed module systems cause runtime errors that are extremely hard to debug
fiveWH.HOW: Reads package.json type field and validates all files follow the declared module system

fiveWH.WHAT: Refactor scan agent — suggests safe structural improvements to code
fiveWH.WHY: Continuous refactoring suggestions keep the codebase clean and maintainable
fiveWH.HOW: Pattern-based heuristics detect long functions, deep nesting, and complex conditionals

fiveWH.WHAT: Syntax agent — guards against malformed code and unsafe patch results
fiveWH.WHY: Malformed syntax breaks builds; this agent catches errors before they reach CI
fiveWH.HOW: Lightweight structural pattern checks for common JS/TS syntax issues

fiveWH.WHAT: Endpoint agent — detects existing health and HTTP endpoints safely
fiveWH.WHY: Prevents duplicate endpoint registration and detects port conflicts before startup
fiveWH.HOW: Reads manifest files and scans source for endpoint declarations

fiveWH.WHAT: Env agent — validates required vs optional environment variables
fiveWH.WHY: Missing or misconfigured env vars cause runtime failures that are hard to debug
fiveWH.HOW: Reads .leeway/env-schema.json and validates against process.env

fiveWH.WHAT: Health agent lite — runs lightweight startup and readiness checks
fiveWH.WHY: Services must pass readiness gates before routing traffic or accepting requests
fiveWH.HOW: Runs a series of configurable health checks, returns pass/fail with details

fiveWH.WHAT: Manifest agent — validates MCP manifest completeness and tool definitions
fiveWH.WHY: Incomplete or malformed manifests prevent MCP servers from being discovered and used
fiveWH.HOW: Reads manifest JSON, validates required fields and tool schemas

fiveWH.WHAT: Port agent — assigns and validates ports from a registry to prevent collisions
fiveWH.WHY: Port collisions cause service startup failures; governance requires a centralized port registry
fiveWH.HOW: Maintains a port registry in .leeway/ports.json, validates before assignment

fiveWH.WHAT: Process agent — handles process ownership, shutdown sequencing, and zombie cleanup
fiveWH.WHY: Unmanaged processes cause resource leaks, port conflicts, and failed restarts
fiveWH.HOW: Tracks PID registry in .leeway/processes.json, validates liveness, signals shutdown

fiveWH.WHAT: Runtime agent — checks module and runtime compatibility before build and start
fiveWH.WHY: Runtime version mismatches cause failures that are difficult to diagnose in CI
fiveWH.HOW: Reads package.json engines field and compares against current Node version

fiveWH.WHAT: Transport agent — verifies approved MCP transport usage
fiveWH.WHY: MCP transport mismatches cause silent failures and security violations
fiveWH.HOW: Checks transport declarations in manifests and source for approved protocols

fiveWH.WHAT: Doctor agent — runs a full system health and compliance diagnosis
fiveWH.WHY: The entire LEEWAY system must be diagnosable in a single command
fiveWH.HOW: Orchestrates assess, audit, runtime, env, and health checks into one unified report

fiveWH.WHAT: Memory agent lite — stores SDK-local state, receipts, audit history, and registries
fiveWH.WHY: Agent operations must be logged and recoverable; receipts provide auditability
fiveWH.HOW: Persistent JSON store in .leeway/memory.json with receipt logging

fiveWH.WHAT: Router agent — chooses which other agents to invoke for a given task
fiveWH.WHY: Complex governance tasks require orchestrated invocation of multiple specialized agents
fiveWH.HOW: Intent-based routing from task descriptions to named agent handlers

fiveWH.WHAT: Permission agent — checks action permissions and runtime boundaries
fiveWH.WHY: Actions must be gated by explicit permission checks to prevent unauthorized operations
fiveWH.HOW: Checks declared AGENTS list in headers and validates against permission rules

fiveWH.WHAT: Policy agent — enforces LEEWAY policy bundles across the codebase
fiveWH.WHY: Governance requires consistent enforcement of named policy sets
fiveWH.HOW: Loads policy bundles from .leeway/policies.json, evaluates against file metadata

fiveWH.WHAT: Privacy agent — checks privacy and data handling declarations in code
fiveWH.WHY: Systems handling personal data must declare their data handling intent
fiveWH.HOW: Scans for PII patterns in logs and data structures, checks for privacy declarations

fiveWH.WHAT: Prompt security agent — scans prompt files and agent instructions for unsafe patterns
fiveWH.WHY: Malicious prompt injection can compromise AI agent behavior and expose sensitive operations
fiveWH.HOW: Pattern matching against known prompt injection techniques and unsafe instruction patterns

fiveWH.WHAT: Secret scan agent — looks for secrets and unsafe credential handling in source code
fiveWH.WHY: Committed secrets are a critical security vulnerability; this agent prevents them
fiveWH.HOW: Pattern matching against known secret formats, env var names, and credential patterns

fiveWH.WHAT: Tool access agent — ensures tools are called only through approved paths
fiveWH.WHY: Unapproved tool invocation paths create unauditable side effects and security gaps
fiveWH.HOW: Validates tool call paths against an approved routes registry

fiveWH.WHAT: Authority agent — declares allowed actions, denied actions, and execution scope per file/module
fiveWH.WHY: Governance requires explicit declaration of what each file is permitted to do
fiveWH.HOW: Reads LEEWAY headers and .leeway/authority.json to validate action permissions

fiveWH.WHAT: Discovery pipeline agent — ensures every artifact has the discovery chain metadata
fiveWH.WHY: LEEWAY applications must be visible to search engines, AI tools, and voice assistants
fiveWH.HOW: Checks for Schema.org JSON-LD, OpenGraph, sitemap, and FAQ structured data presence

fiveWH.WHAT: Header agent — inserts and repairs LEEWAY headers in source files
fiveWH.WHY: All LEEWAY files must have a valid identity header; this agent enforces that rule
fiveWH.HOW: Reads files, detects missing or malformed headers, inserts or repairs as needed

fiveWH.WHAT: Placement agent — checks file placement against LEEWAY directory structure rules
fiveWH.WHY: Files in wrong directories break discoverability and architecture legibility
fiveWH.HOW: Compares declared REGION against expected directory paths, reports misplacements

fiveWH.WHAT: Region agent — assigns REGION correctly to files based on path and content
fiveWH.WHY: Files must belong to the correct system region for governance and discoverability
fiveWH.HOW: Path analysis and content scanning to determine the most appropriate region

fiveWH.WHAT: Registry agent — updates tag registry, file registry, and system map
fiveWH.WHY: A LEEWAY system needs a queryable registry of all files and their identities
fiveWH.HOW: Walks codebase, reads LEEWAY headers, writes to .leeway/registry.json

fiveWH.WHAT: Tag agent — infers TAG values from file path, purpose, and role
fiveWH.WHY: Every file needs an accurate TAG for the system to be machine-searchable
fiveWH.HOW: Uses path heuristics and file content analysis to suggest optimal TAGs

fiveWH.WHAT: LEEWAY CLI — the primary command-line interface for the LEEWAY governance SDK
fiveWH.WHY: Developers need a single command to run governance checks, audits, and repairs
fiveWH.HOW: Node.js CLI using process.argv, no heavy dependencies, direct agent invocation

fiveWH.WHAT: Scores LEEWAY compliance for files, directories, and repositories
fiveWH.WHY: The LEEWAY system must be able to quantify how well a codebase follows governance standards
fiveWH.HOW: Weighted scoring across header, tag, placement, security, and naming rules

fiveWH.WHAT: Unit tests for the LEEWAY compliance-scorer core module
fiveWH.WHY: Compliance scoring drives audit reports and governance gates
fiveWH.HOW: Node.js built-in test runner (node:test) with assert

fiveWH.WHAT: Parses, validates, and builds LEEWAY identity headers from source files
fiveWH.WHY: Every LEEWAY-compliant file must begin with a structured identity header; this module reads and writes those headers
fiveWH.HOW: Pure JS string parsing, no regex over-engineering, line-by-line state machine

fiveWH.WHAT: Unit tests for the LEEWAY header-parser core module
fiveWH.WHY: The header parser is foundational — every other module depends on it working correctly
fiveWH.HOW: Node.js built-in test runner (node:test) with assert

fiveWH.WHAT: Classifies files into LEEWAY system regions
fiveWH.WHY: Every file must belong to a named region that defines its role and layer in the system
fiveWH.HOW: Path-based heuristics with configurable overrides

fiveWH.WHAT: Unit tests for the LEEWAY region-classifier core module
fiveWH.WHY: Region classification determines where files belong; errors affect entire governance pipeline
fiveWH.HOW: Node.js built-in test runner (node:test) with assert

fiveWH.WHAT: Validates and infers LEEWAY TAG values for files
fiveWH.WHY: Every file needs a structured TAG to be discoverable and machine-readable
fiveWH.HOW: Pattern matching against known domain/subdomain/asset/purpose vocabulary

fiveWH.WHAT: Unit tests for the LEEWAY tag-validator core module
fiveWH.WHY: TAG validation is critical — invalid tags break discoverability and governance
fiveWH.HOW: Node.js built-in test runner (node:test) with assert

fiveWH.WHAT: LEEWAY SDK main entry point — exports all agents, core modules, and utilities
fiveWH.WHY: Provides a single importable surface for consumers of the LEEWAY governance framework
fiveWH.HOW: ESM exports, lazy agent loading, zero heavy dependencies
